title: Quota Enforcement Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the quota enforcement capability, recovered from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage, not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facet descriptions are concise source-backed summaries citing verified manifest rows; proof is hermetic/stale, not current readiness. Enrich facet detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: The quota lifecycle treats no configured quota as opt-in allowed, lets an operator set a quota with audit-before-upsert, evaluates entitlement before quota on each check, aggregates current usage, and returns a within/exceeded/no-entitlement/no-quota decision, throwing a typed denial for disallowed actions. Delta-aware checks include the requested increment (for example upload byte size) so a request that would cross the limit is denied before it is applied.

stateModel.description: State is a quota record (quota key, entitlement key, meter key, limit, window, deny action, updated-at/updated-by) plus a computed evaluation state of within, exceeded, no-entitlement, or no-quota, together with current usage, an allowed flag, and a projected usage value for delta checks. Usage is derived from the metering aggregate rather than stored independently.

permissions.description: Tenant quota reads are tenant-scoped and operator set/update operations require operator authorization for a specified tenant; evaluation additionally requires the configured entitlement. Cross-tenant quota access is prevented and policy mutation is operator-only.

contracts.description: The interface is a quota repository port exposing quota read, operator set/update, and an evaluation/assert operation that returns or enforces the allow/deny decision. The contract orders entitlement evaluation before quota comparison and supports projected-usage deltas for operations that add usage.

validation.description: Operator routes validate tenant identifiers and the set-quota request schema; the use case treats absent quota as opt-in allowed, validates entitlement before any usage comparison, and evaluates deltas for usage-adding operations. The entitlement-before-usage ordering is a load-bearing invariant.

errorModel.description: An assert operation throws a typed denial once a quota is exceeded or the entitlement is revoked, while evaluation returns explicit denied decisions distinguishing no-entitlement from exceeded; no-quota resolves to allowed. Failures fail closed and do not grant usage on ambiguous provider state.

auditModel.description: Setting a quota emits an audit record before the mutation (audit-before-upsert) carrying the acting operator, and denial decisions are observable for enforcement. The quota resource is identified explicitly in audit metadata.

readinessModel.description: Readiness is proven against live storage covering entitlement-before-quota ordering, the exceeded state, typed denial via assert, revoke-driven denial, and the no-quota allow path, with route handlers validated separately. When the quota substrate is unavailable, mutation paths are disabled and readiness degrades explicitly.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by quota unit tests and runtime proofs validating live entitlement-before-quota ordering, exceeded state, typed denial, revoke denial, and route handlers. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model before generation.
